forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,49 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '9380'
+title: 'EditMatch: Unicode confusable-punctuation rung + confusable diagnostics in near-miss diffs'
+---
+## What
+
+Port grok/codex apply_patch's pass-4 matcher behavior (see `xai-org/grok-build`, `seek_sequence.rs`) into our recovery ladder: an ASCII-authored `find` must match source text containing typographic punctuation (en/em dashes, smart quotes, exotic spaces) — the single most common real-world edit-match failure. Two facets, one concern: fold confusables when matching, and name confusables when a near-miss diff still fails.
+
+**1. `Sources/FileTool/EditMatch.swift` — new ladder rung**
+
+- Add a static `foldConfusables(_ text: String) -> String` that maps, per Unicode scalar:
+  - dashes `U+2010`–`U+2015`, `U+2212` → `-`
+  - single quotes `U+2018`–`U+201B` → `'`
+  - double quotes `U+201C`–`U+201F` → `"`
+  - spaces `U+00A0`, `U+2000`–`U+200A`, `U+202F`, `U+205F`, `U+3000` → space
+  - everything else unchanged. Make it `package`/`public` visible — `EditEngine` needs it for the diagnostic facet.
+- Add `Rung.unicode`. Ladder order becomes exact → normalized → **unicode** → anchor → fuzzy (order is the contract; the new rung must only fire when `normalized` has descended).
+- Implement the rung by generalizing the existing `locateLineBlock` line-comparison machinery to take a line normalizer, so the unicode rung is `locateLineBlock` with `normalize ∘ foldConfusables` — do not duplicate the window-scan loop. Uniqueness/ambiguity flows through the existing `finalizeBlockMatches` (one match → `.unique(range:rung:confidence: 1.0)`, several → `.ambiguous`, none → descend).
+- Do **not** change the exact, normalized, anchor, or fuzzy rungs (fuzzy scoring stays confusable-blind): the Rust-parity golden vectors in `Tests/FileToolTests/Fixtures/edit-match-golden.json` must pass byte-identical. Update the `EditMatch` header doc: the ladder is now five rungs, and the unicode rung is a deliberate extension beyond the Rust `swissarmyhammer-edit-match` crate (flag for future upstream parity).
+
+**2. `Sources/FileTool/EditEngine.swift` — confusable diagnostic in near-miss diffs**
+
+When the ladder still fails (the span differs by more than punctuation), the near-miss diff currently shows a smart-quote line and an ASCII-quote line as an opaque expected/actual pair. In the diff construction (`diff(expected:actual:)` / `nearMiss(for:span:)`): when an `expected` line and an `actual` line differ but are equal after `normalize` + `EditMatch.foldConfusables`, surface a diagnostic. Add an optional `note: String?` to `EditEngine.NearMiss` (default `nil`) naming the first confusable difference with its code point, e.g. `differs only by Unicode punctuation: the file has '\u{2019}' (U+2019) where the find has "'" (U+0027)`.
+
+**3. `Sources/FileTool/Operations/EditFile.swift` — wire projection**
+
+Project the new note onto `EditNearMiss` as an optional `note` field (nil-omitted like the other optional outcome fields). No status/`matchedBy` changes: a unicode-rung match already surfaces as `recovered` via the existing `EditEngine.Resolution` mapping.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] A `find` written with ASCII `-`, `'`, `"`, and plain spaces resolves to a `.unique` match with rung `.unicode` and confidence `1.0` against content containing en/em dashes, smart quotes, and no-break/thin spaces, with the returned byte range indexing the original (typographic) bytes.
+- [ ] Several confusable-folded occurrences yield `.ambiguous(candidates:)` — never a silent first pick.
+- [ ] Ladder precedence holds: content containing both a verbatim occurrence and a confusable variant resolves at `.exact`, not `.unicode`.
+- [ ] All existing golden vectors in `Tests/FileToolTests/Fixtures/edit-match-golden.json` pass unchanged.
+- [ ] A near-miss whose diff contains a confusable-equal expected/actual line pair carries a `note` naming the code point; a near-miss without confusable differences carries `note == nil`.
+- [ ] `EditFile` output for such a near-miss includes the note on the `EditNearMiss` wire entry.
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] `Tests/FileToolTests/EditMatchTests.swift`: new unicode-rung suite — en dash (U+2013), em dash (U+2014), minus (U+2212), smart single quote (U+2019), smart double quotes (U+201C/U+201D), NBSP (U+00A0) and thin space (U+2009); an ambiguity case (two folded occurrences); a precedence case (exact occurrence elsewhere wins over a confusable variant); `foldConfusables` unit cases including identity on ASCII.
+- [ ] `Tests/FileToolTests/EditEngineTests.swift`: near-miss with a confusable-equal line pair yields the note; near-miss with a genuinely different line yields `note == nil`.
+- [ ] `Tests/FileToolTests/EditFileTests.swift`: one end-to-end case asserting the note appears in the encoded `EditNearMiss`.
+- [ ] `swift test` — full suite green.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file
reverse_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,49 +0,0 @@
----

-title: 'EditMatch: Unicode confusable-punctuation rung + confusable diagnostics in near-miss diffs'
----
-## What
-
-Port grok/codex apply_patch's pass-4 matcher behavior (see `xai-org/grok-build`, `seek_sequence.rs`) into our recovery ladder: an ASCII-authored `find` must match source text containing typographic punctuation (en/em dashes, smart quotes, exotic spaces) — the single most common real-world edit-match failure. Two facets, one concern: fold confusables when matching, and name confusables when a near-miss diff still fails.
-
-**1. `Sources/FileTool/EditMatch.swift` — new ladder rung**
-
-- Add a static `foldConfusables(_ text: String) -> String` that maps, per Unicode scalar:
-  - dashes `U+2010`–`U+2015`, `U+2212` → `-`
-  - single quotes `U+2018`–`U+201B` → `'`
-  - double quotes `U+201C`–`U+201F` → `"`
-  - spaces `U+00A0`, `U+2000`–`U+200A`, `U+202F`, `U+205F`, `U+3000` → space
-  - everything else unchanged. Make it `package`/`public` visible — `EditEngine` needs it for the diagnostic facet.
-- Add `Rung.unicode`. Ladder order becomes exact → normalized → **unicode** → anchor → fuzzy (order is the contract; the new rung must only fire when `normalized` has descended).
-- Implement the rung by generalizing the existing `locateLineBlock` line-comparison machinery to take a line normalizer, so the unicode rung is `locateLineBlock` with `normalize ∘ foldConfusables` — do not duplicate the window-scan loop. Uniqueness/ambiguity flows through the existing `finalizeBlockMatches` (one match → `.unique(range:rung:confidence: 1.0)`, several → `.ambiguous`, none → descend).
-- Do **not** change the exact, normalized, anchor, or fuzzy rungs (fuzzy scoring stays confusable-blind): the Rust-parity golden vectors in `Tests/FileToolTests/Fixtures/edit-match-golden.json` must pass byte-identical. Update the `EditMatch` header doc: the ladder is now five rungs, and the unicode rung is a deliberate extension beyond the Rust `swissarmyhammer-edit-match` crate (flag for future upstream parity).
-
-**2. `Sources/FileTool/EditEngine.swift` — confusable diagnostic in near-miss diffs**
-
-When the ladder still fails (the span differs by more than punctuation), the near-miss diff currently shows a smart-quote line and an ASCII-quote line as an opaque expected/actual pair. In the diff construction (`diff(expected:actual:)` / `nearMiss(for:span:)`): when an `expected` line and an `actual` line differ but are equal after `normalize` + `EditMatch.foldConfusables`, surface a diagnostic. Add an optional `note: String?` to `EditEngine.NearMiss` (default `nil`) naming the first confusable difference with its code point, e.g. `differs only by Unicode punctuation: the file has '\u{2019}' (U+2019) where the find has "'" (U+0027)`.
-
-**3. `Sources/FileTool/Operations/EditFile.swift` — wire projection**
-
-Project the new note onto `EditNearMiss` as an optional `note` field (nil-omitted like the other optional outcome fields). No status/`matchedBy` changes: a unicode-rung match already surfaces as `recovered` via the existing `EditEngine.Resolution` mapping.
-
-## Acceptance Criteria
-
-- [ ] A `find` written with ASCII `-`, `'`, `"`, and plain spaces resolves to a `.unique` match with rung `.unicode` and confidence `1.0` against content containing en/em dashes, smart quotes, and no-break/thin spaces, with the returned byte range indexing the original (typographic) bytes.
-- [ ] Several confusable-folded occurrences yield `.ambiguous(candidates:)` — never a silent first pick.
-- [ ] Ladder precedence holds: content containing both a verbatim occurrence and a confusable variant resolves at `.exact`, not `.unicode`.
-- [ ] All existing golden vectors in `Tests/FileToolTests/Fixtures/edit-match-golden.json` pass unchanged.
-- [ ] A near-miss whose diff contains a confusable-equal expected/actual line pair carries a `note` naming the code point; a near-miss without confusable differences carries `note == nil`.
-- [ ] `EditFile` output for such a near-miss includes the note on the `EditNearMiss` wire entry.
-
-## Tests
-
-- [ ] `Tests/FileToolTests/EditMatchTests.swift`: new unicode-rung suite — en dash (U+2013), em dash (U+2014), minus (U+2212), smart single quote (U+2019), smart double quotes (U+201C/U+201D), NBSP (U+00A0) and thin space (U+2009); an ambiguity case (two folded occurrences); a precedence case (exact occurrence elsewhere wins over a confusable variant); `foldConfusables` unit cases including identity on ASCII.
-- [ ] `Tests/FileToolTests/EditEngineTests.swift`: near-miss with a confusable-equal line pair yields the note; near-miss with a genuinely different line yields `note == nil`.
-- [ ] `Tests/FileToolTests/EditFileTests.swift`: one end-to-end case asserting the note appears in the encoded `EditNearMiss`.
-- [ ] `swift test` — full suite green.
-
-## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file

+  text: 'Picked up by /finish. No deps (extends done EditMatch 9gpx... err EditMatch ydsaz6b + EditEngine ph3qbq5 + EditFile 0efws09). Port grok/codex apply_patch pass-4 confusable matcher. (1) EditMatch.swift: add package/public foldConfusables(_:)->String (per-scalar: dashes U+2010-2015,U+2212→-; single quotes U+2018-201B→''; double U+201C-201F→"; spaces U+00A0,U+2000-200A,U+202F,U+205F,U+3000→space; else unchanged); add Rung.unicode; ladder order exact→normalized→UNICODE→anchor→fuzzy (order is the contract, unicode fires only after normalized descends); implement by GENERALIZING existing locateLineBlock to take a line-normalizer → unicode rung = locateLineBlock w/ normalize∘foldConfusables (do NOT duplicate window-scan); uniqueness via existing finalizeBlockMatches (1→.unique rung:.unicode confidence 1.0, several→.ambiguous, none→descend). Do NOT change exact/normalized/anchor/fuzzy (fuzzy stays confusable-BLIND) — golden vectors edit-match-golden.json MUST pass byte-identical; update header doc (5 rungs, unicode is deliberate extension beyond Rust crate, flag for upstream parity). (2) EditEngine.swift: add optional note:String?=nil to NearMiss; in diff(expected:actual:)/nearMiss(for:span:), when expected+actual lines differ but are equal after normalize+foldConfusables, set note naming first confusable diff w/ code point (e.g. "differs only by Unicode punctuation: the file has ''\u{2019}'' (U+2019) where the find has \"''\" (U+0027)"). (3) EditFile.swift: project note onto EditNearMiss optional note (nil-omitted); no status/matchedBy change (unicode match surfaces as recovered via existing Resolution mapping). Acceptance: ASCII find→.unique .unicode 1.0 vs typographic content, range indexes ORIGINAL bytes; several folded→.ambiguous never silent pick; precedence — verbatim occurrence wins at .exact over confusable variant; golden unchanged; confusable-equal near-miss pair→note w/ code point, non-confusable→note==nil; EditFile encodes note. Front-load conventions + data-driven confusable map. Starting implement→test→commit→review loop.'

Precedence holds (exact rung runs first). EditEngine.swift: NearMiss gained optional note; nearMiss(for:span:) sets it via confusableNote scanning expected/actual line pairs that are confusable-fold-equal, naming the first differing scalar with U+XXXX code points. EditFile.swift: EditNearMiss gained optional note (nil-omitted), projected in nearMissOutput. swift test: 283 unit tests / 19 suites + 26 integration tests / 7 suites all green; new unicode/foldConfusables/ambiguity/precedence/near-miss-note/EditFile-note tests pass; golden parity suite green. Left in doing for review.'

Acceptance: ASCII find→.unique .unicode 1.0 vs typographic content, range indexes ORIGINAL bytes; several folded→.ambiguous never silent pick; precedence — verbatim occurrence wins at .exact over confusable variant; golden unchanged; confusable-equal near-miss pair→note w/ code point, non-confusable→note==nil; EditFile encodes note. Front-load conventions + data-driven confusable map. Starting implement→test→commit→review loop.'

+- actor: claude-code
+  id: 01kxqe12nvfzkwc6dt3dmt5cz5
+  text: 'Adversarial double-check found a real defect in the near-miss note logic: confusableNote fired a false/misattributed note when a near-miss line pair differed by indentation (whitespace) rather than confusables — the gate accepted trim-equal pairs and firstDifferingScalar compared untrimmed lines, naming e.g. a space vs a letter. Fixed TDD (two failing regression tests first): gate is now isConfusableDifference = trimmed(find) != trimmed(file) AND foldedForComparison(find) == foldedForComparison(file) (folding must be load-bearing), and firstConfusableScalar aligns on the TRIMMED lines and only reports a divergence when the two scalars fold to the same ASCII form (a genuine confusable pair) — otherwise nil. Added tests: indentationOnlyNearMissHasNoConfusableNote (note nil) and indentedConfusableNearMissNamesTheConfusableNotTheIndentation (names U+2019/U+0027 despite leading indentation). swift test now 285 unit / 19 suites + 26 integration / 7 suites, all green; golden fixture still unchanged.'
